function.arguments: {"cards":[1,4,5],"reasoning":"To improve the potential score of the Flush hand without risking the ability to play it. By discarding the three non-Heart cards, we draw three new cards, which may include additional Hearts to replace low-value cards in the Flush (H_2 and H_4), potentially increasing the score above 288 and possibly exceeding 300 in one hand. This maintains the guaranteed minimum of 288 while offering upside potential. The Ace of Diamonds is discarded as it doesn't contribute to the Heart Flush, and we can afford to cycle it for this opportunity in the easy Ante 1 Small Blind."}
function.name: discard

function.arguments: {"cards":[2,4,5],"reasoning":"We currently have a strong Heart Flush option with H_A, H_K, H_T, H_4, H_2 (values 11+10+10+4+2=37 chips + 35 base = 72 total chips × 4 mult = 288 score). This is very close to the 300 target. The three non-Heart cards (C_J, S_9, S_8) don't contribute to this Flush and can be safely discarded to cycle for potentially higher-value Hearts or other useful cards. This discard preserves all Hearts while drawing 3 new cards, giving a chance to replace the low H_4 and H_2 (total 6 chips) with better options, possibly pushing the Flush over 300 in one hand. Worst case, we fall back to the original 288 and use remaining hands for the difference. This is efficient use of our first discard in Ante 1 Small Blind, where we have plenty of hands (4) and discards (3) to work with."}
function.name: discard

function.arguments: {"cards":[0,1,4,5,6],"reasoning":"The current hand contains six Hearts: H_A (11), H_K (10), H_T (10), H_6 (6), H_4 (4), H_2 (2). Playing the five highest-value Hearts forms a Flush: base 35 chips + 41 from cards = 76 chips × 4 mult = 304 score. This exceeds the 300 target for the Small Blind in Ante 1 using just one hand, preserving all remaining hands (3) and discards (2) for maximum money from unspent hands ($1 per unused hand). No Jokers or enhancements are present, so this is a straightforward high-score play. The non-Heart cards (C_K, D_J) and low H_2 are held back as they don't contribute to this Flush's score, but we'll draw 5 new cards after playing, refreshing the hand for the next phase."}
function.name: play

function.arguments: {"card":1,"reasoning":"Scary Face is an excellent early-game Joker that provides +30 Chips per played face card, directly aligning with the strategy of obtaining a solid Chips Joker in Antes 1-3. Face cards (J/Q/K) are common in the standard deck, and this will help boost scores for the upcoming Big Blind (450 chips required), where my previous plain Flush scored 304. This purchase strengthens immediate scoring potential while leaving enough money for another investment."}
function.name: buy

function.arguments: {"pack":0,"reasoning":"In early Ante 1 after Small Blind, with $6 and Scary Face already providing good chip bonuses for face cards, the priority is building a solid foundation by acquiring more Jokers for synergy and scaling. The Buffoon Pack at $4 guarantees options for up to 2 Jokers, allowing selection of one that complements Scary Face (ideally another chip or mult booster for face cards or general hands). This leaves $2 for potential future use, better than spending all $6 on Satellite (which requires Planet usage for value and provides no immediate scoring help) or rerolling (which costs $5 and risks worse options). Spectral Pack is too high-risk early without a clear strategy needing its effects. This aligns with early-game focus on Joker collection while preserving some economy."}
function.name: buy

function.arguments: {"card":1,"reasoning":"In early Ante 1 with Scary Face providing +30 Chips per played face card, selecting Crafty Joker builds synergy for a Flush-focused strategy, which is reliable and aligns with the recent successful Heart Flush play. Crafty adds +80 Chips to Flush hands, significantly boosting scores for the upcoming Big Blind (450 required) - for example, turning a 76-chip Flush into 156 chips ×4 = 624, easily clearing it. This complements Scary Face as Flushes can include face cards. Wrathful Joker is too suit-specific (Spades only) and provides smaller +3 Mult per card, less impactful without Spade focus. Skipping wastes the Buffoon Pack opportunity when we have Joker slots available (1/5) and need to collect synergistic Jokers for foundation building in Antes 1-3."}
function.name: pack

function.arguments: {"reasoning":"We currently have $2, which is insufficient to purchase the Satellite Joker ($6), Wasteful Voucher ($10), or Spectral Pack ($4), or to reroll ($5). Our existing Jokers—Scary Face (+30 Chips for played face cards) and Crafty Joker (+80 Chips for Flush)—provide a solid foundation for early Ante 1, synergizing well with Flush hands that include face cards, as demonstrated by our successful Small Blind play. Selling a Joker to afford the Spectral Pack would incur a permanent economic loss (selling at half value) and weaken our scoring potential, violating the core strategy of avoiding unnecessary sales, especially this early when the Jokers align with our emerging Flush-focus. Spectral cards carry high risk (e.g., money loss, hand size reduction) without guaranteed synergy, making them suboptimal for foundation-building in Antes 1-3. Satellite Joker requires Planet usage for value, which we haven't invested in yet. With no consumables to use and no beneficial rearrangements needed (both Jokers provide additive Chips, so order has minimal impact), advancing to the Big Blind (450 chips required) preserves our setup and economy for future shops, where we can earn more from unspent hands and interest."}
function.name: next_round
